titleStmt.title: Letter from John Garvey to the under-secretary, 16 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Garvey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from John Garvey, crown solicitor for County Mayo, to the Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939). In the letter Garvey refers to an earlier communication from the Chief Secretary's Office regarding the intimidation of Colonel Beddington’s workmen and states that the matter will receive attention.Sir Matthew Nathan served as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6812

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Crown Solicitor's Office Ballina 16 April 1916
5934 Sir I beg to acknowledge receipt
of minute of the 15/4/16 re
intimidation of Col Beddingtons
workmen and the matter
will have attention I am Sir
Your obdt servt John Garvey
CS. The Under Secretary The Castle Dublin
